description: Self-contained stereochemistry conformance cases for SynKit. Transformation cases distinguish end-to-end executable, graph-only and isotope-deferred coverage; negative assertions remain specification-only until an annotation/conformance runner is available. CIP descriptors are assigned after atom maps are cleared so map numbers cannot influence ranking. Stable catalog_refs and the deferred catalog_backlog remain embedded in this file for traceability.

notes: local_geometry, descriptor_change and product_population are three independent axes. ST-10 is retained/flipped, ST-03 is inverted/retained, and ST-20 is retained/destroyed; no single verdict per reaction is sufficient.

Never compare @ against @@ across the arrow. Both symbols depend on SMILES neighbour order. Use the mapped-neighbour permutation parity of Section 10, then recompute CIP independently on each side. ST-22 exists to catch exactly this bug.

expected_unique_stereoisomer_count counts distinct product stereoisomers after atom maps are cleared. A fully specified template contributes 1; a count of 2 must come from an explicit RACEMIC entry in rule_metadata.stereo_outcomes, never from the SMILES.
'racemic' asserts a 50:50 composition; 'unknown' asserts that the composition was not determined. They are different claims and must not compare equal. ST-45 and ST-46 test the distinction.

step_relationship alternative_face_rules means the steps are correlated alternatives whose union is the oracle. Branching each stereocentre independently would additionally generate the meso combinations and is wrong; see ST-04 and ST-07.
For multi-step cases the oracle describes the composed sequence, not any single step. ST-11's two inversions compose to net retention.
graph_only means SynKit preserves the declared product descriptor in the ITS/product graph but the current reaction-SMILES export does not preserve it end to end.

Parsing note, verified against RDKit 2026.03.1: Chem.MolFromSmiles silently discards mapped explicit hydrogens that are bound to a heavy atom, so [C@:2]([H:6])(...) loses map 6 while the free [H:6][H:7] survives. Consumers must parse the mapped_reaction fields with SmilesParserParams(removeHs=False), otherwise map conservation appears to fail for every case that tracks a transferred hydrogen (ST-14 through ST-16, ST-19, ST-20, ST-30 through ST-32, ST-37).

cases.name: SN1 solvolysis of (R)-1-phenylethyl chloride

oracle.notes: Substrate configuration fixed to R to agree with the catalog annotation for ST-01/TH-07. The single capture template serialises one enantiomer; the equal pair comes from rule_metadata, not from the SMILES.

cases.name: Ordinary SN2 inversion of (R)-2-bromobutane
SN2 inversion with unchanged R CIP label

oracle.notes: Walden inversion and the Br-to-CN priority reordering cancel at the CIP-label level.

cases.name: Syn deuteration of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Corrected: the correlated R,R/S,S pair needs two deterministic face rules, exactly as in ST-07. A single fully specified template emits one enantiomer only, and independent per-centre branching would also generate the incorrect meso combinations.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentres.

cases.name: Anti bromination of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Single rule suffices: the two face matches give enantiomeric assignments of a meso product, i.e. the same compound.

cases.name: Syn deuteration of Z-but-2-ene

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentres.

cases.name: Anti bromination of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: The correlated R,R/S,S pair is represented by two deterministic face rules; independent per-centre branching would also generate incorrect meso combinations.

cases.name: Fischer esterification of (S)-lactic acid at the carboxyl group

oracle.notes: No bond to the stereocentre is touched; acyl-oxygen cleavage keeps both geometry and label.

cases.name: Remote SN2 that does not change CIP priorities at the stereocentre

oracle.notes: Reaction is two bonds away; the OH > CH2CH2X > CH3 > H ranking survives Br -> CN.

cases.name: Remote substitution that reverses CIP priorities while geometry is retained

oracle.notes: The catalog's formal TH-03 example. CH2Br > CH2Cl becomes CH2Cl > CH2F, so ranks 2 and 3 swap and the label flips although no bond to the centre is broken. A system that infers geometry from the CIP label alone fails here.

cases.name: Two consecutive SN2 reactions at the same centre give net retention

oracle.notes: Oracle is over the composed two-step sequence: inverted then inverted is net retention. Each individual step is inverted; a per-step checker must not collapse the pair into a single retention edit.

cases.name: Enolate-mediated racemization of (S)-2-methylbutanal

oracle.notes: Connectivity is unchanged across the pair; only the stereo element is destroyed and rebuilt. Structurally parallel to ST-01 but with no leaving group, so a checker keyed on connectivity change must still see the stereo edit.

cases.name: Hypochlorite oxidation of (R)-2-butanol to 2-butanone

oracle.notes: Both enantiomers are accepted and converge on the same achiral ketone, so rejected_substrates is deliberately empty.

cases.name: Achiral reduction of 2-butanone creates a racemic centre

oracle.notes: Same graph edit as ST-15/ST-16; only the rule metadata differs. The template serialises one enantiomer, which is not the oracle.

cases.name: Asymmetric reduction of 2-butanone to (R)-2-butanol

oracle.notes: Identical graph edit to ST-14 and ST-16; the facial choice lives in the rule, not in the connectivity.

cases.name: Asymmetric reduction of 2-butanone to (S)-2-butanol

oracle.notes: Enantiomeric catalyst to ST-15; the two cases must not be deduplicated to one rule.

cases.name: Homolysis at a stereogenic carbon flattens the centre

oracle.notes: The sec-butyl radical is treated as planar. Pairs with ST-18; see also NG-06 in the catalog for the cage-model caveat.

cases.name: Radical recombination in an achiral environment recreates a racemate
Enzymatic deuteride delivery creates an isotope-only stereocentre

oracle.notes: (R)-[1-2H]ethanol. C2 carries CH3, OH, H and D, so the centre exists only by virtue of the isotope: the CIP decision falls to Rule 2 (mass number) after Rule 1 ties.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Graph/rewrite conversion emits mapped [2H] as [H], removing the isotope-defined product stereocentre.

cases.name: Making two substituents identical destroys an intact stereocentre

oracle.notes: The hard case for the local_geometry/descriptor split: no bond to C2 is touched, so the local geometry is retained, yet the centre stops being stereogenic because CH2Cl becomes a second CH3. Propan-2-ol is achiral. Systems that equate 'centre destroyed' with 'bond to centre broken' fail here.

cases.name: Azide displacement at one of two stereocentres

oracle.notes: local_geometry/descriptor_change in this oracle refer to the reacting centre atom:4. The spectator centre atom:2 is retained at R and must be reported unchanged; per-molecule diffing that flags 'stereochemistry changed' without localising it fails this case.

cases.name: Exchanged mapping of two equivalent methyls must not report a false inversion

oracle.notes: The two variants are the same reaction; maps 1 and 3 (the two isopropyl methyls) are exchanged in the product of variant_b. Both must yield exactly one identical product and report inverted at atom:5 only. Reporting any change at atom:2 is a false positive.

cases.name: Syn dihydroxylation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Isotope-free analogue of ST-04: same geometry, executable today. AD-03 and ST-04 must agree on the E + syn -> chiral pair outcome even though D is CIP rank 3 while OH is rank 1.

cases.name: Syn dihydroxylation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Isotope-free analogue of ST-06.

cases.name: Epoxidation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: trans-2,3-dimethyloxirane is chiral, so the achiral peracid gives a racemate. The oxygen is delivered suprafacially, hence relative_stereochemistry syn even though the catalog table calls the product 'trans'.

cases.name: Epoxidation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: cis-2,3-dimethyloxirane is meso.

cases.name: Cyclopropanation of E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Singlet carbene adds suprafacially; trans-1,2-dimethylcyclopropane is chiral.

cases.name: Cyclopropanation of Z-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: cis-1,2-dimethylcyclopropane is meso.

cases.name: HBr addition to 1-butene via a planar secondary cation

oracle.notes: Markovnikov regiochemistry plus a racemic centre. The alkene has no E/Z element to destroy, so this isolates centre creation from descriptor destruction.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of 2-phenyl-1-butene with an achiral catalyst

oracle.notes: Prochiral alkene, achiral catalyst: both enantiotopic faces are used. Same graph edit as ST-31.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of 2-phenyl-1-butene with a chiral catalyst

oracle.notes: Identical connectivity change to ST-30; only the facial selectivity annotation differs.

cases.name: Hydrogenation of E-but-2-ene leaves no stereochemical signal

oracle.notes: The E/Z element is destroyed and butane carries no stereo element. Z-but-2-ene is deliberately NOT rejected: it is a legitimate substrate giving the same product, which is what makes this addition non-stereospecific in the observable sense. Contrast ST-04, where the same syn delivery is stereospecific only because the isotope label survives.

cases.name: Remote coupling reverses alkene CIP priority while geometry is retained

oracle.notes: Double-bond analogue of ST-10. On C4 the ranking CH2Br > n-propyl becomes n-propyl > ethyl, so the reference substituent moves to the other side of the double bond and the label flips E -> Z with the geometry untouched.

known_blocker.observed_effect: The product graph retains the planar descriptor, but generated reaction SMILES omits the expected Z descriptor.

cases.name: Photochemical E to Z isomerization of but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Connectivity is completely unchanged; the whole edit is the stereo element. A rule engine keyed on bond changes alone produces an empty ITS here.

cases.name: Z to E isomerization of but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Connectivity is completely unchanged; the whole edit is the stereo element. A rule engine keyed on bond changes alone produces an empty ITS here.

cases.name: Reductive ozonolysis of E-but-2-ene destroys the double-bond stereo

oracle.notes: Z-but-2-ene gives the identical product; the stereo element is destroyed by C=C cleavage rather than by saturation, unlike ST-32.

cases.name: Lindlar semihydrogenation of but-2-yne creates the Z-alkene

oracle.notes: Syn delivery of both hydrogens on the catalyst surface. Same graph edit as ST-38; the geometry comes only from the condition annotation.

cases.name: Dissolving-metal reduction of but-2-yne creates the E-alkene

oracle.notes: Pairs with ST-37: identical connectivity change, opposite geometry, decided by the trans-vinyl-anion intermediate. The two cases must not deduplicate to one rule.

known_blocker.observed_effect: explicit_h=true is required to generate the product; the product graph contains E but generated reaction SMILES is stereo-free.

cases.name: Zinc debromination of meso-2,3-dibromobutane gives E-but-2-ene

oracle.notes: Exact microscopic reverse of ST-05. Two tetrahedral centres are destroyed and one E/Z element is created in a single edit, so local_geometry and descriptor_change refer to different loci: this is the case that breaks any single-valued stereo verdict per reaction.

known_blocker.observed_effect: The product graph retains E, but generated reaction SMILES contains an unspecified double bond.

cases.name: Zinc debromination of RR/SS-2,3-dibromobutane gives Z-but-2-ene

rule_set_metadata.note: Two enantiomeric substrates converge on one achiral product.

oracle.notes: Reverse of ST-07 and the mirror-image test of ST-39. Both enantiomers of the substrate are needed and both give Z; a rule set that keeps only one enantiomer silently halves the substrate scope.

known_blocker.observed_effect: Both product graphs retain Z, but generated reaction SMILES contains an unspecified double bond.

cases.name: SN1 product whose stereochemical composition was never measured

assertion.rationale: Same transformation as ST-01, but sourced from a record with no measured e.e. ST-01 may assert the idealized racemate because its rule_set_metadata declares the 0.5/0.5 population; this case may not. Emitting either C[C@H](O)c1ccccc1 or C[C@@H](O)c1ccccc1 as the product is a failure, and so is silently promoting the stereo-free SMILES to 'racemic'.

cases.name: Flexible E2 elimination without conformational information

assertion.rationale: 3-bromopentane is conformationally free, so anti-periplanar geometry does not fix the alkene descriptor. The E/Z locus must be marked underdetermined rather than defaulted to E on thermodynamic grounds. Contrast with DB-11, which supplies a rigid substrate and is therefore deterministic.

cases.name: Terminal alkene carries no E/Z element

assertion.rationale: C1 bears two hydrogens, so no E/Z element exists. Any directional bond written onto this double bond is meaningless and must not be reported as a descriptor.

cases.name: Alkene carbon with two identical substituents carries no E/Z element

assertion.rationale: C3 of 2-methylbut-2-ene bears two methyls. CIP ranking cannot distinguish them, so the double bond is not stereogenic. This is the duplicate-substituent case of NG-09; ST-43 is the duplicate-hydrogen case.

cases.name: Unreported product stereochemistry must stay unknown, not racemic

assertion.rationale: The graph edit is identical to ST-14/ST-15/ST-16. Those cases may assert racemic or a single enantiomer because their conditions declare the catalyst; this one may not. 'Unknown' and 'racemic' are different claims: the first says the composition was not determined, the second asserts a 50:50 measurement. Collapsing them destroys the distinction the catalog's Section 10 requires.

cases.name: Stereo-free SMILES needs external annotation to mean racemic

assertion.rationale: One and the same product SMILES backs both readings. The two annotations must round-trip to distinct records and must never compare equal. A serializer that writes the ensemble annotation into the SMILES, or a loader that drops it, fails this case. This is the annotation-layer counterpart of ST-45.

cases.name: Simple tertiary amine is not a stable nitrogen stereocentre

assertion.rationale: N-ethyldimethylamine inverts pyramidally far faster than it can be resolved, so the nitrogen must not be treated as a stereocentre even though it formally carries four different ligands counting the lone pair. Contrast HE-06, where quaternization removes the lone pair and creates a genuinely stable centre.

cases.name: Structurally impossible trans-cycloalkene must be rejected or flagged

assertion.rationale: trans-Cyclohexene is not isolable at ordinary conditions; the SMILES is structurally invalid rather than merely unusual. Verified behaviour of RDKit 2026.03.3: both C1/C=C/CCC1 and C1/C=C\CCC1 parse without error and canonicalize to C1=CCCCC1, i.e. the directional bonds are silently discarded rather than flagged. A conformance layer must therefore raise its own diagnostic; relying on the toolkit to reject the input is not sufficient.

catalog_backlog.reason: Needs a multi-centre epimerization substrate with an oracle that localises the single inverted centre; ST-21 covers the weaker 'react at one of two centres' claim.
A literal D-for-H replacement is not a single graph edit; encoding it needs the isotope-aware rewrite that ST-04/ST-06/ST-19 are blocked on.
Prochiral desymmetrization by mono-protection needs prochirality (pro-R/pro-S) annotation, which the schema does not yet carry.
Needs a reaction that maps an RR/SS pair onto a meso product while retaining both centres; no clean small-molecule instance chosen yet.

Net anti dihydroxylation via epoxidation then hydrolysis. Requires composing a face-rule pair (ST-25/ST-26) with an anti ring opening; the composed oracle is the target of a separate rule-composition test.
Halohydrin formation couples stereochemistry to regiochemistry; needs a regio oracle alongside the stereo oracle.
Hydroboration-oxidation of 1-methylcyclohexene is a ring case; blocked with the RG group below.
Trivially subsumed by ST-33 minus the priority reversal; worth adding as the control once ST-33 passes.
Rigid anti-periplanar E2 needs a conformationally locked substrate, i.e. a ring; blocked with RG.
C=N stereochemistry. RDKit assigns E/Z across C=N, but oxime/imine geometry needs its own oracle and lone-pair ranking checks.

Ring cases need cis/trans relative-stereochemistry annotation across a ring and, for RG-11..RG-16, carbohydrate and peptide substrates. None are encoded; fabricating them without an executable oracle would violate the catalog's own entry criterion.

Pericyclic selectivity is not a function of connectivity: conrotatory/disrotatory and endo/exo require an explicit thermal-vs-photochemical field and a facial descriptor the schema does not yet define.
Axial and spiro chirality. RDKit supports allene and atropisomer stereo only partially; needs a decision on representation before an oracle can be trusted.
Heteroatom stereocentres (S, P, Si, N+). Encodable in principle; deferred until tetrahedral carbon passes end to end, per the catalog's own Section 7 instruction.
Pseudoasymmetric r/s centres exercise CIP Rules 4b/5; needs a dedicated oracle since a like/unlike ranking bug would silently pass every case above.
Partially covered by ST-04/ST-06/ST-19 for R/S; the isotope-dependent E/Z half has no case yet.
Enhanced stereo (ABS/AND/OR) groups. ST-46 encodes the racemic-vs-unknown distinction at the annotation layer, but real AND/OR group round-tripping needs an extended-SMILES or MOL V3000 path.
Coordination stereo, marked optional in the catalog.
Wittig and HWE default-geometry tests need ylide-stabilisation context fields; the assertion is about a missing input, so the case must carry the context schema first.
Partially covered by ST-18, which declares 'no cage model' in its conditions. A true NG-06 needs a positive cage-model counterpart to contrast against.

Section 11 asks for 14 serialization variants of every chemically distinct gold reaction. These are generated mechanically from the cases above rather than stored, except for variant 14 (equivalent-atom mapping permutation), which is encoded explicitly as ST-22 because it needs a hand-written second mapping.
